entities.name: sphota
entities.cite: The sphota is abhinna-kala, without temporal division

entities.name: grahaṇopādhi
entities.cite: the conditions under which it is grasped

axioms.name: abhinna-kala
axioms.cite: The sphota is abhinna-kala, without temporal division

axioms.name: dhvani-kala-anupatin
axioms.cite: it is dhvani-kala-anupatin, appearing to run along the timeline of the sounds that manifest it

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: that differs grahana-upadhi-bhedena, according to the conditions under which it is grasped

denials.name: binna-kala
denials.cite: it does not itself take time

denials.name: bheda
denials.cite: what differs is not the sphota but its vrtti

claims.name: dhvani-kala-anupatin
claims.cite: appears to follow the time of the sounds

rejected_readings.label: sphota_temporal
rejected_readings.rendering: The sphota is temporally divisible and takes time like the sounds.
rejected_readings.why: The verse explicitly establishes the sphota is abhinna-kala (timeless). Only the sounds (dhvani) that manifest it have temporal extension. The appearance of temporality is mediated through manifestation, not intrinsic to the sphota.

rejected_readings.label: sphota_mutable
rejected_readings.rendering: The substance of the sphota itself varies according to different utterances and contexts.
rejected_readings.why: The commentary explicitly denies this: 'what differs is not the sphota but its vrtti'. The sphota's substance is invariant; only its mode of functioning (vrtti) differs according to conditions of apprehension.

rejected_readings.label: sounds_determine
rejected_readings.rendering: The sounds (dhvani) determine the word-essence (sphota); they are the source from which it arises.
rejected_readings.why: The relationship is reversed. The sphota is the timeless word-essence that manifests through sounds. The sounds are the manifestation (vivartate from sphota), not the source. Sphota is ontologically primary.

notes: The contract formalizes the central paradox of 1.75: the sphota is intrinsically timeless (abhinna-kala) yet appears to follow the timeline of manifestation (dhvani-kala-anupatin). The resolution is that the sphota's substance remains invariant while its mode of functioning (vrtti) varies according to the conditions of apprehension (grahana-upadhi). This framework grounds the later distinction between prakrta-dhvani and vaikrta-dhvani, allowing grammarians to identify which acoustic differences matter to word identity.
